message:
Last spring I spent maybe five minutes labeling our flashlights and battery packs with a permanent marker. Sounds silly, right? Fast forward to a surprise severe thunderstorm at 2 a.m., and my kid was genuinely calmer reaching for a flashlight that clearly said "WORKS" instead of fumbling through a drawer of mystery devices. That tiny prep moment—just *knowing* what we had and where—turned what could've been chaos into "oh, okay, we're good."

It made me realize that sometimes the biggest wins in storm readiness aren't fancy systems or panic plans. They're just small, thoughtful touches that let everyone breathe a little easier when things get loud and dark.

What's one small-but-mighty prep thing you've done that actually paid off when you needed it?
